pages.raw_text:
1T. the miners made serious objec.
ton to the timbering of the mines
on the grounds that they were not
safe.


Numbers Of the Goldfield mines
have been leased; the leasers hay.
ing time limit, worked on royal.
ty and ran with as small expense
as possible.


This Is the reason offered in sew.
eral instances for numbers of un.
timbered shafts and galleries,
where the miners positively refused
to work after serious cavern re-
sulting in a number OF deaths
Added to this there were frequent
protests from the men who worked
the mines, because of the danger.
Gus manipulation of the cross-heads
of guide bars used in hosting ma
chinery.


The cross-heads are constructed
of wood. In a wet mine they. fire.
quently swell and in several cases
have been known to stick in the
guide near the surface, and the
miner, helpless to stop the cage,
has been lowered to the bottom,
hanging against the sides of the
shaft in deadly peril every moment.
All these and many other things
were the preliminaries to the out
break when the financial stringency
and the scrip issue came and start
ed the strike.
